raw_response: I agree with the final decision but would add another point of clarification. The key function of heparin indeed centers around enhancing antithrombin III's ability to inhibit thrombin, thereby blocking an early and critical step in the coagulation pathway—namely, converting prothrombin to thrombin. However, it also indirectly influences downstream processes where thrombin acts as a catalyst, ensuring no part of the clotting sequence proceeds unchecked once activated. Therefore, while option B accurately captures the essence of hisarins’ primary target, understanding its holistic effect provides deeper insight into its broad-spectrum anti-coagulant properties. My agreement stands firm with the selected answer being B. Answer Choice: B.
